document.name: gan-harness-dapr-showcase
document.title: GAN coding harness (dapr-agent-py): Planner → Generator↔Evaluator contract → build+test-graded loop → PR (openshell-shared, code profiles)
document.summary: Anthropic GAN-style general coding harness running on dapr-agent-py over the openshell-shared workspace backend (sibling of gan-harness-cli-showcase). A workspace/profile provisions ONE shared /sandbox openshell sandbox (keepAfterRun); a deterministic clone_repo node clones repoUrl into /sandbox/repo. The Planner writes a HIGH-LEVEL SPEC.md; the Generator and a skeptical Evaluator NEGOTIATE a rigid acceptance contract (each criterion kind=objective|subjective) before any code. The build loop generates, passes a deterministic gate (build + tests), and the Evaluator grades the RUNNING artifact by executing the suite + exercising the API/endpoints (evaluationProfile=library|service; no browser). Exits when the gate passes AND every criterion passes; outputMode controls PR/branch/none. All durable/run agents + workspace/command nodes share the SAME openshell sandbox via sandboxName/workspaceRef. GITHUB_TOKEN is ambient in the sandbox. ui-web (browser) profile is a documented follow-up.

intent.title: Redesign request
intent.default: Improve input validation and test coverage for the is-number library: correctly classify edge cases (empty string, whitespace-only, hex like "0x1F", scientific notation like "1e3", Infinity, and numeric strings) and keep `npm test` passing. Do NOT change the public API or add dependencies.
intent.description: What the agents change in the repo.

planAgent.title: Planner agent
planAgent.description: PLAN phase agent (reads /sandbox/repo + writes SPEC). openshell-shared family.

criticAgent.title: Critic agent
criticAgent.description: EVALUATOR agent — negotiates the contract AND grades the build by running the test suite / exercising the API (no browser). dapr-agent-py family.

buildCommand.title: Build command
buildCommand.description: Build command the deterministic gate runs ('auto' = detect pnpm/npm/cargo/make).

previewCommand.title: Preview command
previewCommand.description: Preview/serve command the critic views ('auto' = detect npm/pnpm).

designPass.title: Two-pass design
designPass.description: Run the design-tokens+wireframe review BEFORE code ('false' to skip).

criticVotes.title: Critic votes
criticVotes.description: Independent Evaluator votes per build iteration; a criterion passes only if ALL votes agree (1 = single critic).

maxRestarts.title: Max restarts
maxRestarts.description: How many times the Evaluator may force a from-scratch restart (git reset --hard baseline) on fundamentally-broken work.

repoUrl.title: Repository (owner/repo)
repoUrl.description: GitHub owner/repo the agents clone + (optionally) open a PR into.

repoRef.title: Branch
repoRef.description: Branch to clone from and use as the PR base.

evaluationProfile.title: Evaluation profile
evaluationProfile.description: How the Evaluator grounds + grades: 'ui-web' (Playwright + design rubric), 'library' (run tests + exercise the public API), 'service' (boot + hit endpoints + integration tests).

outputMode.title: Output mode
outputMode.description: Remote-write policy: 'pr' (push branch + open PR), 'branch' (push branch only), 'none' (no remote change).

testCommand.title: Test command
testCommand.description: Objective verify the gate runs for code profiles ('auto' = detect npm/pnpm test, cargo test, pytest).

taskScope.title: Task scope
taskScope.default: Edit src/routes/+page.svelte plus a global stylesheet; plain SvelteKit + CSS only; do NOT change package.json deps or routing.
taskScope.description: Free-form hint of what/where to change (replaces UI-file hardcoding).

intent.title: Redesign request
intent.default: Improve input validation and test coverage for the is-number library: correctly classify edge cases (empty string, whitespace-only, hex like "0x1F", scientific notation like "1e3", Infinity, and numeric strings) and keep `npm test` passing. Do NOT change the public API or add dependencies.

with.command:
${ "set -e
cd /sandbox
rm -rf repo
git clone https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/" + (.trigger.repoUrl // "jonschlinkert/is-number") + ".git repo
cd /sandbox/repo
REF=" + ((.trigger.repoRef // "") | @sh) + "
if [ -n "$REF" ] && git rev-parse --verify "origin/$REF" >/dev/null 2>&1; then git checkout "$REF"; fi
git config user.email agent@workflow-builder.local
git config user.name "workflow-builder agent"
BASE=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
printf %s "$BASE" > /sandbox/.wfb_base
echo CLONED $(git rev-parse HEAD) on $BASE" }

agentConfig.name: Planner
agentConfig.instructions: You are the PLANNER for an autonomous coding task. The target repo is ALREADY cloned at /sandbox/repo. READ the repo (entry points, package manifest, existing tests) and write a HIGH-LEVEL spec to /sandbox/SPEC.md (ONLY that file; do NOT edit the repo). Cover: the product/task context, the target user/consumer, and the quality dimensions that matter for THIS task — for a UI task: design_quality, originality, craft, functionality; for a library/service task: correctness, tests, code_quality, maintainability, performance. Deliberately stay HIGH-LEVEL: do NOT enumerate granular, implementation-level acceptance criteria — the Generator and the Evaluator negotiate the specific, testable criteria next. End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Task:
" + (.trigger.intent // "make the requested change") + "

Scope hint: " + (.trigger.taskScope // "") + "

Target repo: https://github.com/" + (.trigger.repoUrl // "jonschlinkert/is-number") + " (branch " + (.trigger.repoRef // "main") + ")" + "
The repo is ALREADY cloned at /sandbox/repo. READ it (entry points, package manifest, existing tests) and write a HIGH-LEVEL spec to /sandbox/SPEC.md (product/task context + the quality dimensions relevant to this task). Do NOT enumerate granular acceptance criteria (those are negotiated next). Do NOT edit the repo. End with a one-line summary." }

design.if: ${ (.approve_goal_spec.timedOut // false) == false and (.approve_goal_spec.approved // true) == true and ((.trigger.designPass // "true") != "false") and ((.trigger.evaluationProfile // "ui-web") == "ui-web") }

agentConfig.name: Designer (Generator, two-pass)
agentConfig.instructions:
You are the GENERATOR doing the FIRST design pass, BEFORE writing any code (no CSS/HTML yet). Read /sandbox/SPEC.md. Produce a compact, OPINIONATED design system and commit to a distinctive direction (avoid generic 'AI slop' — no default purple-gradient-on-white-cards, no near-black + acid-green). Write TWO files at /sandbox (NOT in the repo):
1) /sandbox/design-tokens.json — STRICT JSON: {"palette":[{"name":"...","hex":"#..."}, ... 4-6 named colors], "typography":{"display":"font + role","body":"font + role"}, "spacing":"the scale/rhythm", "motif":"the one bold, justifiable aesthetic risk"}.
2) /sandbox/wireframe.txt — an ASCII wireframe of the page layout (hero, feature cards, pricing, CTA), top to bottom.
On later rounds READ /sandbox/design-review.json (the Evaluator's objections) and REVISE both files to address every objection. Do NOT edit the repo. End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Two-pass design (proposal). Intent: " + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the landing page") + "

Read /sandbox/SPEC.md and, if present, /sandbox/design-review.json (prior objections). Write/revise /sandbox/design-tokens.json (4-6 named hex + display/body type + spacing + one bold motif) and /sandbox/wireframe.txt (ASCII layout). No code." }

agentConfig.name: Design lead (Evaluator, two-pass)
agentConfig.instructions: You are a SKEPTICAL design lead at a small studio reviewing a design PLAN before any code (do NOT open a browser; there is nothing to view yet). Read /sandbox/SPEC.md, /sandbox/design-tokens.json and /sandbox/wireframe.txt. Judge ONLY on Originality and Craft: does the palette/typography/spacing form a coherent, DISTINCTIVE identity grounded in the product+audience, with at least one bold, justifiable aesthetic risk? REJECT generic library-default / 'AI slop' choices (ubiquitous purple gradients over white cards; near-black backgrounds with acid-green accents; default system fonts with no hierarchy). Write /sandbox/design-review.json as STRICT JSON: {"approved": <bool>, "feedback": "specific, actionable objections or what to change"}. Set approved:true ONLY when the direction is distinctive and coherent (not generic) — otherwise approved:false with concrete objections. End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt: ${ "Review the DESIGN PLAN (no code yet). Read /sandbox/SPEC.md, design-tokens.json, wireframe.txt. Write /sandbox/design-review.json {approved, feedback}. Reject generic/AI-slop direction; approve only a distinctive, coherent one." }

with.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do [ -s /sandbox/design-review.json ] && break; sleep 1; done
python3 - <<'PYZZ'
import json,os
P='/sandbox/design-review.json'
try:
    d=json.load(open(P))
except Exception:
    print(json.dumps({'approved':False,'haveTokens':os.path.exists('/sandbox/design-tokens.json'),'error':'design-review.json missing/invalid'}));raise SystemExit
ap=d.get('approved')
if ap is None:
    txt=str(d.get('verdict') or d.get('overall_verdict') or d.get('summary') or d.get('feedback') or '').lower()
    ap=(('approv' in txt) or ('looks good' in txt) or ('lgtm' in txt) or ('accept' in txt)) and not (('reject' in txt) or ('revise' in txt) or ('not approv' in txt))
have=os.path.exists('/sandbox/design-tokens.json')
approved=bool(ap) and have
print(json.dumps({'approved':approved,'haveTokens':have}))
PYZZ

agentConfig.name: Proposer (Generator)
agentConfig.instructions: You are the GENERATOR in a contract NEGOTIATION, BEFORE writing any code. Read /sandbox/SPEC.md. Propose a concrete plan: break the work into a SMALL set of discrete, INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIABLE changes. For EACH, state (a) what you will implement, (b) EXACTLY how it can be verified — using the verification method the prompt specifies for this task's evaluation profile (a browser interaction for UI work; a shell/test command or function-call assertion for code work), and (c) a crisp acceptance criterion. On later rounds, READ /sandbox/contract-review.md (the Evaluator's objections) and REVISE your proposal to address every objection. Write your proposal to /sandbox/proposal.md (ONLY that file; do NOT edit the repo and do NOT write contract.json). End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Contract negotiation (proposal). Intent: " + (.trigger.intent // "make the requested change") + "
Scope: " + (.trigger.taskScope // "") + "
Evaluation profile: " + (.trigger.evaluationProfile // "ui-web") + "

Read /sandbox/SPEC.md and, if it exists, /sandbox/contract-review.md (prior objections). Write/revise /sandbox/proposal.md with discrete changes, each with an acceptance criterion that is " + (if (.trigger.evaluationProfile // "ui-web") == "ui-web" then "BROWSER-verifiable (what a design reviewer sees and clicks on the rendered page)" else "COMMAND/TEST-verifiable — name the EXACT shell/test command or function-call assertion that proves it (e.g. `npm test` passes, `python -m pytest -q` passes, import the module and assert f(x)==expected, or curl an endpoint and assert the status). NO browser checks." end) + "." }

agentConfig.name: Evaluator (Contract negotiation)
agentConfig.instructions: You are a HIGHLY SKEPTICAL EVALUATOR negotiating the acceptance contract for a coding task, BEFORE any code is written. You do NOT write code and you do NOT open a browser yet. Read /sandbox/SPEC.md and /sandbox/proposal.md. Adversarially review the proposed features + acceptance criteria. Reject vague/unmeasurable/overly-broad criteria and ones that omit key edge cases (empty/error states, responsive/mobile layout, keyboard focus / accessibility, interactive elements actually working). Produce a FOCUSED contract — aim for roughly 8-15 of the MOST IMPORTANT, user-visible acceptance criteria (quality over quantity; don't pad with dozens of micro-checks, but don't drop important coverage either). Prefer criteria checkable by THIS task's verification method (the prompt states it): for UI work, perceptual/structural checks a design reviewer makes by looking at and clicking the rendered page (e.g. 'the hero headline is clearly the largest text', 'the primary CTA is visually distinct'); for code work, criteria whose `verify` names the EXACT shell/test command or function-call assertion that proves them (e.g. '`npm test` passes', 'import the module and assert f(x)==expected', 'curl /health → 200') — NOT browser checks. AVOID brittle exact-number assertions (specific pixel sizes / hex / rgb values) where a perceptual description works; describe the INTENT. Write your objections + required changes to /sandbox/contract-review.md. THEN write the negotiated contract to /sandbox/contract.json as JSON of the form {"agreed": <boolean>, "criteria": [{"id":"c1","kind":"objective|subjective","dimension":"<task dimension>","description":"the criterion","verify":"EXACTLY how to check it (a browser interaction for UI; a shell/test command for code)","passes": false}, ...]}. Mark each criterion kind=objective if it is verifiable by running a command/test (it MUST name the command in verify), or kind=subjective if it needs judgment against a rubric. Use task-appropriate dimensions: for UI use design_quality|originality|craft|functionality; for a library/service use correctness|tests|code_quality|maintainability|performance. Give each criterion a clear description + verify and a dimension when you can; every criterion starts with "passes": false. (A deterministic normalizer canonicalizes field names afterward, so focus on clear, testable description+verify rather than exact key names.) CONVERGENCE: set "agreed": true as soon as the criteria are specific, verifiable by the task's method, and cover the key cases — do NOT withhold agreement over minor polish; the build loop iterates on quality. Only keep "agreed": false if criteria are genuinely vague or missing important coverage. End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt: ${ "Negotiate the acceptance contract for the '" + (.trigger.evaluationProfile // "ui-web") + "' profile. Read /sandbox/SPEC.md and /sandbox/proposal.md, write your objections to /sandbox/contract-review.md, and write/update /sandbox/contract.json (agreed + criteria, each kind/dimension/verify, passes:false) as STRICT JSON per your instructions. Each criterion must be " + (if (.trigger.evaluationProfile // "ui-web") == "ui-web" then "BROWSER-verifiable (what a design reviewer sees and clicks on the rendered page)" else "COMMAND/TEST-verifiable — name the EXACT shell/test command or function-call assertion that proves it (e.g. `npm test` passes, `python -m pytest -q` passes, import the module and assert f(x)==expected, or curl an endpoint and assert the status). NO browser checks." end) + " Be skeptical: set agreed:true ONLY when every criterion is specific, verifiable by that method, and covers edge cases." }

with.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do [ -s /sandbox/contract.json ] && break; sleep 1; done
python3 - <<'PYZZ'
import json,re,os
P='/sandbox/contract.json'
_UIDIMS=('design_quality','originality','craft','functionality')
_CODEDIMS=('correctness','tests','code_quality','maintainability','performance')
_ALLDIMS=_UIDIMS+_CODEDIMS
def dim(t):
    t=t.lower()
    if re.search(r'test|spec|coverage|assert|pytest|exit code',t): return 'tests'
    if re.search(r'perf|latency|throughput|benchmark|memory|speed',t): return 'performance'
    if re.search(r'lint|format|typecheck|type-check|readab|docstring|comment',t): return 'code_quality'
    if re.search(r'maintain|modular|coupl|cohes|structure|duplicat',t): return 'maintainability'
    if re.search(r'typograph|spacing|color|contrast|hierarch|whitespace|align|legib',t): return 'craft'
    if re.search(r'distinct|original|custom|bold|unique|generic|template|slop|memorable',t): return 'originality'
    if re.search(r'identity|mood|cohesi|brand|aesthetic|palette|tone|polish',t): return 'design_quality'
    if re.search(r'click|render|responsive|keyboard|accessib|hover|focus|navigat|button|layout|visible',t): return 'functionality'
    return 'correctness'
def kindof(desc,ver):
    t=(str(desc)+' '+str(ver)).lower()
    return 'objective' if re.search(r'\btest\b|command|exit|run |npm |pnpm |cargo |pytest|curl|build|lint|typecheck|assert|returns?\b|==',t) else 'subjective'
try:
    raw=json.load(open(P))
except Exception:
    print(json.dumps({'agreed':False,'criteriaCount':0,'valid':False,'error':'contract.json missing/invalid'}));raise SystemExit
crit=raw.get('criteria') if isinstance(raw.get('criteria'),list) else []
norm=[]
for i,c in enumerate(crit):
    if not isinstance(c,dict): continue
    desc=c.get('description') or c.get('text') or c.get('criterion') or c.get('requirement') or c.get('name') or ''
    ver=c.get('verify') or c.get('verification') or c.get('check') or c.get('how') or ''
    dd=c.get('dimension')
    if dd not in _ALLDIMS: dd=dim(str(desc)+' '+str(ver))
    kk=c.get('kind') if c.get('kind') in ('objective','subjective') else kindof(desc,ver)
    norm.append({'id':str(c.get('id') or ('c'+str(i+1))),'kind':kk,'dimension':dd,'description':str(desc),'verify':str(ver),'passes':bool(c.get('passes'))})
def _nk(s): return ' '.join(re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9 ]',' ',str(s).lower()).split())
seen=set(); dd2=[]
for c in norm:
    k=_nk(c['description'])
    if k and k in seen: continue
    if k: seen.add(k)
    dd2.append(c)
removed=len(norm)-len(dd2); norm=dd2
_MAXC=int(os.environ.get('WFB_MAX_CRITERIA','24') or 24)
capped=max(0,len(norm)-_MAXC); norm=norm[:_MAXC]
valid=len(norm)>=3 and all(x['description'] for x in norm)
agreed=bool(raw.get('agreed')) and valid
raw['criteria']=norm; raw['agreed']=agreed
json.dump(raw,open(P,'w'))
print(json.dumps({'agreed':agreed,'criteriaCount':len(norm),'deduped':removed,'capped':capped,'valid':valid}))
PYZZ

refine.while: ${ ( (( ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS")) and (((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.meets_criteria // false)) == true) ) | not) and ((((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.stalled // false)) == true) | not) ) }

agentConfig.name: Generator
agentConfig.instructions: You are the GENERATOR. Implement the redesign in /sandbox/repo to satisfy the NEGOTIATED CONTRACT. GETTING UP TO SPEED (do this FIRST, every turn): read /sandbox/progress.json (the chronological log of prior iterations) and run `git -C /sandbox/repo log --oneline -10`. If /sandbox/design-tokens.json and /sandbox/wireframe.txt exist, treat them as the AUTHORITATIVE visual direction (color tokens, typography, spacing, the bold motif, layout) — implement THAT design; do not invent a different palette/type system. Then read /sandbox/contract.json (the AUTHORITATIVE acceptance criteria — each has id/description/verify/passes) and /sandbox/SPEC.md. Focus on the criteria whose "passes" is false. Edit whatever files the task requires (see the task scope in the prompt). Prefer the smallest change that satisfies the FAILING criteria; do NOT add dependencies unless the task explicitly calls for it. You do NOT grade your own work: a deterministic build gate + an independent skeptical Evaluator (who RUNS/EXERCISES the artifact per the evaluation profile and grades EACH contract criterion) evaluate every attempt. Each turn: address the build output AND the Evaluator's per-criterion feedback, make the most impactful improvement toward the FAILING criteria, dependencies are PRE-INSTALLED (node_modules present) so run the project's build yourself to verify before finishing, end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Task: " + (.trigger.intent // "make the requested change") + "
Scope: " + (.trigger.taskScope // "") + "

Repo: /sandbox/repo. Authoritative acceptance criteria: /sandbox/contract.json. Spec: /sandbox/SPEC.md. Memory: /sandbox/progress.json (read it + git log FIRST)." + "

Your previous attempt:
" + (.loop.last.generate.content // "(first attempt)") + "

Build gate (must end OBJECTIVE PASS):
" + ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") // "(not run yet)") + "

Evaluator per-criterion feedback (from the browser):
" + (try ((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.feedback // .summary // "(none)")) catch "(none)") + "

Make the build pass AND satisfy every FAILING contract criterion, then end with a brief summary." }

agentConfig.name: Critic
agentConfig.instructions:
You are an exacting, SKEPTICAL EVALUATOR with strong taste, independent of the builder and of any other evaluator; you do NOT edit the repo. GRADE the running app against the negotiated contract.

STEP 1 — start the app. The build already ran (node_modules + build output exist in /sandbox/repo). Start the preview server on port 4173 in the background and wait until it answers:
  cd /sandbox/repo && (nohup npm run preview -- --port 4173 --host 127.0.0.1 >/tmp/preview.log 2>&1 &) ; for i in $(seq 1 40); do curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:4173/ >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 1; done ; tail -5 /tmp/preview.log
If preview never answers, fall back to dev on the same port and wait the same way.

STEP 2 — inspect with the Playwright MCP browser tools (server 'playwright'): browser_navigate to http://127.0.0.1:4173/ , browser_snapshot, browser_take_screenshot (filename 'critic-shot.png' -> /sandbox/critic-shot.png). Resize the viewport (desktop/tablet/mobile) and browser_click the nav / primary CTA, re-snapshotting, to test interactivity + responsiveness. Use the BROWSER — do not write your own screenshot scripts.

IGNORE churn in package-lock.json, node_modules/, .svelte-kit/, build/. Judge ONLY the redesign source (src/) and the rendered UI.

STEP 3 — read /sandbox/contract.json (the AUTHORITATIVE criteria), /sandbox/SPEC.md, /sandbox/design-tokens.json (the agreed visual direction), and the source.

STEP 4 — grade the rendered app against EACH criterion. Decide passes true/false based on what you ACTUALLY SAW / INTERACTED WITH (use the criterion's "verify"). DEFAULT to passes:false unless the evidence is clear. Generic or merely 'fine' work does NOT pass.

STEP 5 — WRITE your verdict to /sandbox/verdict-0.json as STRICT JSON with EXACTLY this shape: {"criteria": [{"id": "c1", "passes": <bool>, "feedback": "concrete browser evidence"}, ...], "allPass": <bool>, "score": <0-100>, "meets_criteria": <same as allPass>, "recommend_restart": <bool — TRUE ONLY if the work is FUNDAMENTALLY broken (build/page does not render, core layout entirely absent) so starting over beats patching; else false>, "feedback": "overall summary of what still fails"}. Write ONLY verdict-0.json (do NOT edit contract.json — a deterministic aggregator combines the votes). After writing it, stop the server (pkill -f vite 2>/dev/null; pkill -f 'npm run' 2>/dev/null) and end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Grade the redesigned app in /sandbox/repo against EACH criterion in /sandbox/contract.json by VIEWING + interacting with it in the browser (Playwright MCP), then WRITE /sandbox/verdict-0.json (per-criterion passes + allPass + meets_criteria + recommend_restart). Follow your instructions exactly." + "

Preview command for THIS run: " + (.trigger.previewCommand // "auto") }

agentConfig.name: Code critic
agentConfig.instructions:
You are an exacting, SKEPTICAL EVALUATOR independent of the builder and of any other evaluator; you do NOT edit the repo and you do NOT open a browser. GRADE the artifact in /sandbox/repo against the negotiated contract by RUNNING it (objective ground-truth), per the evaluation profile in the prompt.

STEP 1 — read /sandbox/contract.json (the AUTHORITATIVE criteria; each has id/kind/dimension/description/verify), /sandbox/SPEC.md, and the relevant source.

STEP 2 — GROUND IN REALITY (never grade from a static diff):
  - library profile: run the project's test suite + typecheck/lint (e.g. `npm test`, `cargo test`, `python -m pytest -q`), and EXERCISE the public API yourself in a scratch script (import it, call it with real + edge inputs, print results). Use the criterion's `verify` command where given.
  - service profile: build + START the server in the background, wait for it to listen, then HIT its endpoints with curl (status codes, happy-path + error/edge payloads) and run any integration tests. Stop the server when done.
  Capture real command output/exit codes as your evidence.

STEP 3 — grade EACH criterion. For kind=objective, it passes ONLY if the named command/test actually exits 0 / produces the asserted output (you ran it). For kind=subjective, judge against the dimension (correctness/code_quality/maintainability/performance) from what you SAW running + reading the code. DEFAULT to passes:false unless the evidence is clear; merely 'looks fine' does NOT pass.

STEP 4 — WRITE your verdict to /sandbox/verdict-0.json as STRICT JSON: {"criteria": [{"id": "c1", "passes": <bool>, "feedback": "concrete command output / evidence"}, ...], "allPass": <bool>, "score": <0-100>, "meets_criteria": <same as allPass>, "recommend_restart": <bool — TRUE only if fundamentally broken (does not build/import/run) so starting over beats patching; else false>, "feedback": "overall summary of what still fails"}. Write ONLY verdict-0.json (do NOT edit contract.json — a deterministic aggregator combines the votes). End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt: ${ "Grade the artifact in /sandbox/repo against EACH criterion in /sandbox/contract.json by RUNNING it (tests / API / endpoints) per the '" + (.trigger.evaluationProfile // "library") + "' profile, then WRITE /sandbox/verdict-0.json (per-criterion passes + allPass + meets_criteria + recommend_restart). Follow your instructions exactly. Test command hint: " + (.trigger.testCommand // "auto") }

agentConfig.name: Critic (vote 2)
agentConfig.instructions:
You are an exacting, SKEPTICAL EVALUATOR with strong taste, independent of the builder and of any other evaluator; you do NOT edit the repo. GRADE the running app against the negotiated contract.

STEP 1 — start the app. The build already ran (node_modules + build output exist in /sandbox/repo). Start the preview server on port 4173 in the background and wait until it answers:
  cd /sandbox/repo && (nohup npm run preview -- --port 4173 --host 127.0.0.1 >/tmp/preview.log 2>&1 &) ; for i in $(seq 1 40); do curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:4173/ >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 1; done ; tail -5 /tmp/preview.log
If preview never answers, fall back to dev on the same port and wait the same way.

STEP 2 — inspect with the Playwright MCP browser tools (server 'playwright'): browser_navigate to http://127.0.0.1:4173/ , browser_snapshot, browser_take_screenshot (filename 'critic-shot.png' -> /sandbox/critic-shot.png). Resize the viewport (desktop/tablet/mobile) and browser_click the nav / primary CTA, re-snapshotting, to test interactivity + responsiveness. Use the BROWSER — do not write your own screenshot scripts.

IGNORE churn in package-lock.json, node_modules/, .svelte-kit/, build/. Judge ONLY the redesign source (src/) and the rendered UI.

STEP 3 — read /sandbox/contract.json (the AUTHORITATIVE criteria), /sandbox/SPEC.md, /sandbox/design-tokens.json (the agreed visual direction), and the source.

STEP 4 — grade the rendered app against EACH criterion. Decide passes true/false based on what you ACTUALLY SAW / INTERACTED WITH (use the criterion's "verify"). DEFAULT to passes:false unless the evidence is clear. Generic or merely 'fine' work does NOT pass.

STEP 5 — WRITE your verdict to /sandbox/verdict-1.json as STRICT JSON with EXACTLY this shape: {"criteria": [{"id": "c1", "passes": <bool>, "feedback": "concrete browser evidence"}, ...], "allPass": <bool>, "score": <0-100>, "meets_criteria": <same as allPass>, "recommend_restart": <bool — TRUE ONLY if the work is FUNDAMENTALLY broken (build/page does not render, core layout entirely absent) so starting over beats patching; else false>, "feedback": "overall summary of what still fails"}. Write ONLY verdict-1.json (do NOT edit contract.json — a deterministic aggregator combines the votes). After writing it, stop the server (pkill -f vite 2>/dev/null; pkill -f 'npm run' 2>/dev/null) and end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Grade the redesigned app in /sandbox/repo against EACH criterion in /sandbox/contract.json by VIEWING + interacting with it in the browser (Playwright MCP), then WRITE /sandbox/verdict-1.json (per-criterion passes + allPass + meets_criteria + recommend_restart). Follow your instructions exactly." + "

Preview command for THIS run: " + (.trigger.previewCommand // "auto") }

agentConfig.name: Code critic (vote 2)
agentConfig.instructions:
You are an exacting, SKEPTICAL EVALUATOR independent of the builder and of any other evaluator; you do NOT edit the repo and you do NOT open a browser. GRADE the artifact in /sandbox/repo against the negotiated contract by RUNNING it (objective ground-truth), per the evaluation profile in the prompt.

STEP 1 — read /sandbox/contract.json (the AUTHORITATIVE criteria; each has id/kind/dimension/description/verify), /sandbox/SPEC.md, and the relevant source.

STEP 2 — GROUND IN REALITY (never grade from a static diff):
  - library profile: run the project's test suite + typecheck/lint (e.g. `npm test`, `cargo test`, `python -m pytest -q`), and EXERCISE the public API yourself in a scratch script (import it, call it with real + edge inputs, print results). Use the criterion's `verify` command where given.
  - service profile: build + START the server in the background, wait for it to listen, then HIT its endpoints with curl (status codes, happy-path + error/edge payloads) and run any integration tests. Stop the server when done.
  Capture real command output/exit codes as your evidence.

STEP 3 — grade EACH criterion. For kind=objective, it passes ONLY if the named command/test actually exits 0 / produces the asserted output (you ran it). For kind=subjective, judge against the dimension (correctness/code_quality/maintainability/performance) from what you SAW running + reading the code. DEFAULT to passes:false unless the evidence is clear; merely 'looks fine' does NOT pass.

STEP 4 — WRITE your verdict to /sandbox/verdict-1.json as STRICT JSON: {"criteria": [{"id": "c1", "passes": <bool>, "feedback": "concrete command output / evidence"}, ...], "allPass": <bool>, "score": <0-100>, "meets_criteria": <same as allPass>, "recommend_restart": <bool — TRUE only if fundamentally broken (does not build/import/run) so starting over beats patching; else false>, "feedback": "overall summary of what still fails"}. Write ONLY verdict-1.json (do NOT edit contract.json — a deterministic aggregator combines the votes). End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt: ${ "Grade the artifact in /sandbox/repo against EACH criterion in /sandbox/contract.json by RUNNING it (tests / API / endpoints) per the '" + (.trigger.evaluationProfile // "library") + "' profile, then WRITE /sandbox/verdict-1.json (per-criterion passes + allPass + meets_criteria + recommend_restart). Follow your instructions exactly. Test command hint: " + (.trigger.testCommand // "auto") }

with.command:
set +e
MAXR=$(cat /sandbox/.wfb_maxrestarts 2>/dev/null || echo 2)
export WFB_MAXRESTARTS="$MAXR"
python3 - <<'PYZZ'
import json,os,subprocess
try: MAXR=int(os.environ.get('WFB_MAXRESTARTS','2') or 2)
except Exception: MAXR=2
try: p=json.load(open('/sandbox/progress.json'))
except Exception: p={'log':[],'baseline':'unknown'}
log=p.get('log') or []
last=log[-1] if log else {}
rc=int(p.get('resetCount',0))
base=p.get('baseline','')
if last.get('recommendRestart') and not last.get('allPass') and rc<MAXR and base and base!='unknown':
    subprocess.run(['git','-C','/sandbox/repo','reset','--hard',base])
    subprocess.run(['git','-C','/sandbox/repo','clean','-fd'])
    p['resetCount']=rc+1
    p.setdefault('log',[]).append({'iteration':len(p.get('log',[])),'event':'restart','resetCount':rc+1,'note':'Evaluator forced from-scratch restart (recommend_restart)'})
    json.dump(p,open('/sandbox/progress.json','w'))
    print('RESTARTED reset_count='+str(rc+1))
else:
    print('NO_RESTART reset_count='+str(rc)+' max='+str(MAXR))
PYZZ

artifacts.title: Rendered redesign (Playwright critic screenshot)
artifacts.description: Full-page screenshot the critic captured in-browser via the Playwright MCP server.

artifacts.title: Negotiated acceptance contract
artifacts.description: The Generator↔Evaluator negotiated contract.json (testable criteria + per-criterion passes flags).

with.command:
REPO=$(git -C /sandbox/repo remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null | sed -e 's#.*github.com[:/]##' -e 's#\.git$##'); [ -n "$REPO" ] || REPO=$(cat /sandbox/.wfb_repo 2>/dev/null || echo PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo)
BASE=$(cat /sandbox/.wfb_base 2>/dev/null || echo main)
OUT=$(cat /sandbox/.wfb_out 2>/dev/null || echo branch)
TITLE=$(head -c 72 /sandbox/.wfb_intent 2>/dev/null || echo 'automated change'); [ -n "$TITLE" ] || TITLE='automated change'
cd /sandbox/repo && git config user.email agent@workflow-builder.local && git config user.name 'workflow-builder agent'
BR=wfb-$(date +%s) && git checkout -b "$BR" && git add -A && (git commit -m "$TITLE (workflow-builder GAN harness)" || echo nothing-to-commit)
git push https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/$REPO.git HEAD:"$BR"
if [ "$OUT" = pr ]; then
  PR=$(curl -sS -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" -H 'Accept: application/vnd.github+json' https://api.github.com/repos/$REPO/pulls -d "{\"title\":\"$TITLE (automated)\",\"head\":\"$BR\",\"base\":\"$BASE\",\"body\":\"Automated change via the workflow-builder GAN coding harness (plan/negotiate/generate/evaluate).\"}")
  echo "$PR" | python3 -c 'import sys,json;d=json.load(sys.stdin);print("PR_URL="+str(d.get("html_url") or d.get("message") or d))'
else echo "BRANCH_PUSHED=$BR"; fi

set.terminalState: ${ if ( ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS")) and (((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.meets_criteria // false)) == true) ) then "satisfied" elif (((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.stalled // false)) == true) then "stalled_no_progress" else "max_iterations_reached" end }
